title: Digital Marketing
text: This page highlights applied digital marketing projects that combine strategy, analytics, SEO, content creation, campaign optimization, and audience engagement. My work focuses on connecting marketing decisions with measurable performance outcomes through data, storytelling, and digital platforms.

title: Digital Marketing
text:
ODI Grips Instagram Analytics Dashboard
This project analyzed ODI Grips’ Instagram performance using Quarto dashboards and marketing analytics techniques. The dashboard summarizes engagement trends, media performance, caption strategy effectiveness, and top-performing content across Instagram campaigns.

What I Did

Cleaned and structured Instagram performance data using R
Built a Quarto dashboard with professional visualizations
Analyzed engagement metrics such as likes, comments, reach, and impressions
Compared content performance across media types and caption strategies
Identified trends that could improve future social media campaigns



Digital Marketing Relevance
This project shows how social media analytics can support better content planning. Instead of guessing what works, the dashboard turns Instagram performance data into clear insights that can guide future campaign strategy.


Skills Demonstrated

Social media analytics
Quarto dashboard development
KPI reporting
Data visualization
Instagram engagement analysis
Marketing performance storytelling

title: Digital Marketing
text:
TRI Off-Page SEO Strategy
For this project, my team developed an off-page SEO strategy for the Trauma Resource Institute, a nonprofit organization focused on trauma-informed care. The project analyzed backlinks, competitor SEO performance, brand consistency, and guest posting opportunities.

My Role
I focused primarily on backlink strategy and competitive SEO research.


Key Contributions

Analyzed TRI’s backlink profile
Compared competitors’ referral domains and authority
Identified toxic backlink risks
Recommended guest posting and outreach opportunities
Connected SEO recommendations to long-term organic visibility



Key Findings

TRI had thousands of backlinks, but backlink quality varied
Competitors had stronger backlink diversity and authority
Partnership outreach could improve TRI’s search visibility
Guest posting could help TRI earn more relevant referral traffic



Digital Marketing Relevance
SEO is one of the most important long-term growth strategies in digital marketing. This project shows how backlink quality, content partnerships, keyword strategy, and domain authority can influence organic traffic.


Skills Demonstrated

SEO auditing
SEMrush and Moz analysis
Competitive research
Backlink strategy
Technical SEO
Digital brand strategy

title: Digital Marketing
text:
Public Safety Awareness Campaign
As part of my work with the Los Angeles Police Protective League, I help create and publish social media content that communicates public safety issues, law enforcement updates, and community awareness messages.
This campaign focused on a man who faced sentencing after assaulting a federal officer during a protest. The purpose of the post was to communicate a clear public safety message: people can disagree politically, protest, and express frustration, but violence against law enforcement officers carries serious legal consequences.

What I Did
Content Strategy
Developed a timely post around a current public safety issue to increase awareness and audience engagement.
Message Framing
Helped frame the post in a way that was direct, informative, and focused on legal consequences rather than political opinion.
Platform Execution
Published the content for Instagram, where visual impact, headline clarity, and caption structure are important for audience attention.
Audience Engagement
The post generated strong engagement, including more than 1,000 likes and active comment discussion.


Why We Posted It
The post was created to remind the public that federal law applies to everyone. Protest activity does not exempt someone from consequences if they assault an ICE agent, federal officer, or any other law enforcement officer.
The broader message was simple: people can disagree with an agency, law enforcement, or government action, but physically attacking officers can lead to prosecution and prison time.


Digital Marketing Relevance
This project shows how social media can be used for public awareness, issue-based communication, and audience education. It also demonstrates the importance of timing, visual design, caption writing, and message clarity when posting about sensitive public safety issues.


Skills Demonstrated

Social media content strategy
Public safety communication
Instagram caption writing
Audience engagement analysis
Timely content publishing
Message framing for sensitive topics

title: Digital Marketing
section: Center for Customer Insights and Digital Marketing
text:
Center for Customer Insights and Digital Marketing
I served as the Digital Marketing Implementations Lead at the Center for Customer Insights and Digital Marketing at Cal Poly Pomona. In this role, I supported digital marketing campaigns, workshops, email marketing efforts, website updates, and performance tracking.
This experience helped me move beyond classroom theory and apply digital marketing tools in a real organizational setting.

What I Did
Campaign Management
Managed social media and email marketing campaigns for workshops, events, and research-related promotions.
Email Marketing
Helped improve email campaign performance by supporting A/B testing, list targeting, and message optimization.
HubSpot Analytics
Used HubSpot analytics to evaluate email performance, including open rates, click-through rates, bounce rates, and audience engagement.
Website Support
Assisted with Cascade CMS website structure updates and content implementation tasks.
Workshop Promotion
Helped promote digital marketing workshops and research seminars through email and social media campaigns.


Results and Impact

Email open rates reached approximately 33%
Click-through rates reached approximately 4.2%
Bounce rates decreased by about 14%
Workshop registrations increased through campaign promotion
Social media KPIs improved over a one-year period



Digital Marketing Relevance
This role helped me build experience in real-world digital marketing implementation. I worked with email campaigns, social media promotion, audience targeting, website updates, and marketing analytics. More importantly, I learned how digital marketing decisions are connected to performance metrics.


Skills Demonstrated

Email marketing
HubSpot analytics
Campaign implementation
Social media promotion
Marketing performance tracking
Website content management
Digital marketing operations

title: Marketing Analytics
text: This page highlights applied analytics projects focused on marketing performance, consumer behavior, predictive modeling, customer retention, and data-driven decision-making. These projects show how I use analytics to turn raw data into business recommendations.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
ODI Grips Instagram Engagement Analysis
This project analyzed Instagram content performance for ODI Grips by comparing caption themes, engagement rate, posting share, and total engagement contribution.

What I Did

Categorized Instagram posts into content themes
Compared engagement rate across caption and content categories
Built a performance matrix to show both efficiency and scale
Identified which content types created the strongest engagement return



Marketing Analytics Relevance
This project shows how marketing analytics can guide content strategy. Instead of guessing what type of content performs best, the analysis uses engagement data to show which themes are worth prioritizing.


Key Insight
Athlete-focused and event-driven content produced the strongest engagement return, while product-heavy content appeared less efficient compared with its share of total posting volume.

title: Marketing Analytics
text: Featured Visual

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Skincare Packaging Research Project
This project studied how cosmetic packaging design affects consumer perceptions of product quality, visual appeal, and brand tier. The study compared luxury, middle-tier, and drugstore packaging styles using survey responses and statistical testing.

What I Did

Helped design a randomized Qualtrics survey
Analyzed over 100 consumer responses
Used ANOVA, MANOVA, chi-square testing, and post-hoc comparisons
Evaluated how visual packaging cues influenced brand perception



Marketing Analytics Relevance
This project connects survey research with brand strategy. It shows how marketers can use data to understand whether packaging communicates the intended brand position before a customer even tries the product.


Key Insight
Packaging design significantly influenced how consumers judged quality, appeal, and brand tier. Luxury packaging generally performed best on appeal, while some drugstore packaging was perceived as more premium than expected.

title: Marketing Analytics
text: Featured Visual

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Bike Rental Forecasting Project
This project used machine learning to predict bike rental demand based on weather, seasonality, holidays, working days, humidity, windspeed, and temperature.

What I Did

Cleaned and prepared a tidy bike rental dataset
Built predictive models using Random Forest and Linear Regression
Evaluated model performance using prediction metrics
Identified which variables had the strongest influence on rental demand



Marketing Analytics Relevance
Predictive modeling is useful in marketing because it helps businesses forecast customer behavior. This project shows how data can support demand planning, campaign timing, and operational decisions.


Key Insight
Temperature, seasonality, and weather conditions were important predictors of bike rental demand. More comfortable conditions were generally associated with higher rental activity.

title: Marketing Analytics
text: Featured Visual

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Telco Customer Churn Report
This project analyzed customer churn using binary logistic regression. The goal was to identify which customer characteristics made people more or less likely to leave the company.

What I Did

Prepared customer churn data for modeling
Created dummy variables for categorical predictors
Built logistic regression models in SPSS
Compared model accuracy and significant predictors
Interpreted churn drivers from a business perspective



Marketing Analytics Relevance
Churn analysis is one of the most valuable uses of marketing analytics. It helps companies identify at-risk customers, improve retention strategy, and focus marketing efforts on the groups most likely to leave.


Key Insight
Customers with longer tenure and longer contracts were less likely to churn. Customers with certain internet services, paperless billing, and shorter-term contracts showed higher churn risk.

title: Marketing Analytics
text: Featured Visual

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
I am currently pursuing a Master of Science in Digital Marketing at Cal Poly Pomona, with a focus on marketing analytics, dashboard reporting, digital strategy, and data storytelling.
My portfolio is designed for employers and professionals looking for someone who can connect marketing strategy with data-driven decision-making. I am interested in roles related to marketing analytics, performance marketing, digital strategy, campaign reporting, and content performance analysis.

title: Jeffrey Hsu
text:
Career Focus
I want to build a career where I can use data to improve marketing decisions. My strongest interest is in helping organizations understand what is working, what is underperforming, and where they should focus next.
I am especially interested in marketing performance dashboards, attribution analysis, website traffic analytics, SEO and paid media reporting, and turning technical analysis into clear business recommendations that teams can actually use.

My digital marketing work focuses on campaign strategy, content planning, SEO, audience targeting, and customer engagement.



Marketing Analytics

Presentation Work
